playbook.intent: Identify a recurring delivery bottleneck, scope a contractor role, source 5–10 candidates, pick one, sign a contractor agreement, and run them through onboarding so they ship billable work in week 3.
playbook.scope: Identify a recurring delivery bottleneck, scope a contractor role, source 5–10 candidates, pick one, sign a contractor agreement, and run them through onboarding so they ship billable work in week 3.

success-criteria.criterion: All 6 stages have written outputs in the project record
Each stage's decision gate was answered before advancing (yes / no in writing)
Final stage produced the required written decision artifact
Every methodology reference loaded cleanly via `faion get-content`

stage.name: Diagnose Bottleneck
stage.intent: Identify the recurring delivery bottleneck a contractor will solve.

tasks.task: Map the last 3 projects' time logs to find repeated overflow tasks
Quantify hours-per-month lost to the bottleneck
Decide: contractor vs automation vs decline-the-work

stage.decision-gate: Advance only if ≥40 hrs/month is consistently lost and contractor is the chosen lever.

stage.name: Scope Role
stage.intent: Write a tight contractor role that the bench can deliver against.

tasks.task: Draft a 1-page contractor job description with outputs not duties
Set a fixed monthly engagement envelope and rate band
Define the paid test task and its scoring rubric

stage.decision-gate: Advance when JD fits on one page AND test task is reproducible across candidates.

stage.name: Source 5-10
stage.intent: Run a small structured sourcing batch.

tasks.task: Post in 2 channels + ask 5 trusted contacts for referrals
Screen for portfolio match and timezone fit before any interview
Send the paid test task to top 3-5 candidates

stage.decision-gate: Advance if ≥2 candidates cleared the test task with a score above the rubric bar.

stage.name: Interview & Decide

tasks.task: Run a 30-min structured interview using the STAR framework
Reference-check at least one past client
Sign a contractor agreement matching jurisdiction

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when contract is countersigned and start date is on calendar.

stage.name: Onboard
stage.intent: First week sets the tone; ship billable work in week 3.

tasks.task: Run the onboarding runbook: access, docs, comms norms, weekly cadence
Pair on one real piece of work in week 1
Assign the first billable deliverable for week 3

stage.decision-gate: Advance when contractor ships the week-3 deliverable to a client without rework.

stage.name: 30-day Review
stage.intent: Lock or unwind the engagement on evidence.

tasks.task: Score the contractor against the JD outputs at day 30
Run a frank 1:1 on what's working and what isn't
Decide: continue / change scope / end engagement

stage.decision-gate: Required output: a written continue / change / end decision.
